title: papaver lapponicum subsp. jugoricum
intro: Papaver lapponicum subsp. jugoricum, also known as the Laponian poppy or Jugovic's poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family, Papaveraceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its alkaloid content, including papaverine and other isoquinoline alkaloids. The health benefits of Papaver lapponicum subsp. jugoricum include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water to create a medicinal tea, or by using the plant's extracts in ointments and salves.
